user_query:
You're my email marketing consultant for a 6-week B2B SaaS launch. Please read all of the following inputs under input/ and build a complete, execution-ready email marketing plan and assets.

Inputs to read:
- input/audience.csv — subscriber roster with fields (email,name,timezone,customer,last_open_date,last_click_date,last_purchase_date,preferences)
- input/domain_setup.txt — current domain/auth status and list health metrics
- input/goals.yaml — launch goals, primary/secondary KPIs, and constraints
- input/content_assets.md — value props, case studies, FAQs, and offers to use in copy
- input/compliance_regions.json — subscriber counts by region (e.g., EU vs US) for compliance considerations

Deliverables to create under output/:
1) output/strategy/plan.md — a 6-week email marketing strategy that integrates:
   - Deliverability plan (domain authentication: SPF, DKIM, DMARC), IP warming schedule if applicable, and blacklist/complaint monitoring
   - List health targets using benchmark thresholds (open rate >20%, click rate >2%, bounce rate <2%, unsubscribe <0.5%, complaints <0.1%) and how we’ll reach them
   - Segmentation approach by behavior and customer status, including a sunset policy for unengaged subscribers
   - Sending cadence with rationale (days/times), and how it adapts for B2B audiences
   - A/B testing plan (one variable at a time, sample sizing) and success metrics
   - Compliance posture for CAN-SPAM and GDPR (include physical mailing address requirement and one-click unsubscribe)

2) output/deliverability/dkim_spf_dmarc.txt — a deliverability checklist that:
   - Audits the current state from input/domain_setup.txt and calls out gaps for SPF, DKIM, DMARC
   - Proposes an “IP warming” plan starting at 50–100 emails/day with ~20% daily increases, and lists blacklist/complaint monitoring actions

3) output/segments/segments.json — machine-readable definition of minimum viable behavior-based segments you will use, with criteria referencing fields from input/audience.csv. Include at least: new_subscribers, engaged_30d, unengaged_90d, customers, vip, and a sunset_policy with days >= 90. Each segment must include a clear "criteria" object referencing fields (e.g., last_open_date, last_click_date, customer, last_purchase_date, preferences, timezone).

4) output/sequences/welcome_sequence.md — a 5–7 email welcome sequence over ~2 weeks that follows best practices. Each email should include: Day number, subject line (40–50 chars), preview text, body outline with one primary CTA, and timing. Avoid spam trigger language (no ALL CAPS, no multiple exclamation points, avoid words like "free" or "act now").

5) output/sequences/reengagement.md — a 3-email re-engagement mini-sequence for unengaged_90d with opt-down and easy unsubscribe options called out.

6) output/ab_testing/subject_tests.csv — at least 8 A/B subject line tests with columns: test_id, variant (A/B), subject_line, preview_text, char_count. Ensure every subject line has char_count <= 50 and avoid spam triggers (no ALL CAPS, no "FREE", no "Act now", no "!!!"). Include varied hypotheses (e.g., personalization vs value prop, curiosity vs clarity).

7) output/schedule/send_calendar.csv — a send calendar for the first 3 launch weeks with columns: date,local_time,segment,campaign_name,timezone. Include at least 12 scheduled sends covering different segments and reflecting B2B-friendly timing (e.g., mid-morning Tue–Thu). Do not include any dates in the past; use relative planning language in plan.md and list concrete rows here with realistic times.

8) output/checklist/compliance.md — a compliance checklist that explicitly covers: physical address in footer, one-click unsubscribe that works within 10 days, honoring opt-outs immediately, GDPR consent and data deletion, and separate consent for marketing vs transactional email.

General requirements:
- Use behavior-based segmentation and include a sunset policy for unengaged after at least 90 days.
- Subject lines must be 40–50 characters and avoid spam triggers and excessive punctuation.
- Make sure all file paths and filenames match exactly as listed. Do not write anywhere outside output/.
- Ground recommendations in the supplied inputs and explicitly reference any assumptions you make.

When finished, ensure all specified files are created under output/ with complete content.
